AlignmentPeriodCalendar.description:
- UNSPECIFIED: No value has been specified.
 - CALENDAR_MINUTE: Align at minute boundaries
 - CALENDAR_HOUR: Align at hour boundaries
 - CALENDAR_DAY: Align at day boundaries
 - CALENDAR_WEEK: Align at week boundaries (where beginning of the week is monday)
 - CALENDAR_MONTH: Align at month boundaries
 - CALENDAR_QUARTER: Align at quarter boundaries
 - CALENDAR_YEAR: Align at year boundaries

FieldFilterOperatorFunction.description:
- NONE: No function will be applied to the values
 - ANY: Any value in the list must pass the filter
 - ALL: All values in the list must pass the filter

GroupByDimension.title: Dimension specifies which column to group by

GroupByExcludeCombination.title:
ExcludeCombination allows a user using ROLLUP OR CUBE to exclude a combination from the result
Example: CUBE (pid, agid) returns the combinations ((pid, agid), (pid), (agid), ()). Providing
ExcludeCombination{dimensions=[pid, agid]} would return the combinations ((pid), (agid), ())

GroupByLimit.title: Limit reduces the number of dimensions to the specified amount

MeasureAggregate.description: Aggregate applies a SQL aggregation function to a measure field.

alias.description:
Optional. An alias for the aggregated result in the response schema.
Useful when you have multiple aggregates on the same measure with different filters.

measure.description: The measure field to aggregate. Must be one of the metric's available measures.

MeasureAggregateOperator.description:
- AVG: Calculate the average value.
 - COUNT: Count the number of rows.
 - SUM: Sum all values.
 - MIN: Get the minimum value.
 - MAX: Get the maximum value.
 - COUNT_DISTINCT: Count unique values.
 - ARRAY_AGG: Collect all values into an array.

column.title: Either a dimension or a measure to order by

PropertyPIITag.description:
PIITag defines potential PII tags.

 - PROPERTY_PII_TAG_CLEAN: clean means the data is not PII or PD.
 - PROPERTY_PII_TAG_PII: PII means Personally Identifiable Information. It is information that can be used to retrieve personal data, such as IAM user ID.
 - PROPERTY_PII_TAG_PD: PD means personal data, such as name, email and address. They are sensitive data we need to protect in our system.

v1Alignment.description:
- ALIGN_NONE: No alignment.
 - ALIGN_DELTA: Align and convert to delta metric type. This alignment is valid
for delta metrics. Aligning an existing delta metric to a delta metric
requires that the alignment period be increased. The value type of the
result is the same as the value type of the input.

One can think of this aligner as a rate but without time units; that
is, the output is conceptually (second_point - first_point).
 - ALIGN_INTERPOLATE: Align by interpolating between adjacent points around the
period boundary. This alignment is valid for gauge
metrics with numeric values. The value type of the result is the same
as the value type of the input.
 - ALIGN_NEXT_OLDER: Align by shifting the oldest data point before the period
boundary to the boundary. This alignment is valid for gauge
metrics. The value type of the result is the same as the
value type of the input.
 - ALIGN_EXACT: Align by using data points that fall on the same UTC day of the period boundary.
This alignment is valid for daily gauge metrics.
The value type of the result is the same as the value type of the input.
 - ALIGN_CUSTOM: Allows the user to specify custom alignment logic, allowing the query to make more complex decisions on which
time/date bucket(s) a row should be placed in.
This is useful when your table has multiple columns that need to be considered when bucketing rows into time
buckets, such as a created and deleted date.
The custom aligner allows you to bucket these rows based on their status at one of the bucket boundaries or
within the bucket.

v1BusinessResourceId.description: A single business location identified by its account group ID.

businessId.description: The account group ID of the business (e.g., "AG-XXXXXXXX").

v1CompositeFilter.description: A filter that merges multiple other filters using the given operator.

filters.description:
The list of filters to combine.
Must contain at least one filter.

v1CompositeFilterOperator.description:
A composite filter operator.

 - OPERATOR_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified. This value must not be used.
 - AND: The results are required to satisfy each of the combined filters.
 - OR: The results are required to satisfy at least one of the combined filters.

skipFilteringEntitiesToDateRange.title:
Whether to skip filtering entities by the time column on the table to the provided date_range.
Usage: date_range is used for both filtering entities and defining the time period to aggregate over.
If true, date_range will only be used to define the aggregation time range (i.e. the last year)
If false, date_range will be used for BOTH filtering entities AND the aggregation time range

v1DateRange.title: Specifies a range of time for analytics queries

comparisonField.title: A field to compare the dimension to

operatorFunctionOnField.title: Whether to apply the specified operator function to the field, or the value involved in the filter

v1FieldFilter.title: A filter on a specific field

v1FieldFilterOperator.description:
- OPERATOR_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified. This value must not be used.
 - LESS_THAN: Less than. Requires that the field come first in `order_by`.
 - LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL: Less than or equal. Requires that the field come first in `order_by`.
 - GREATER_THAN: Greater than. Requires that the field come first in `order_by`.
 - GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL: Greater than or equal. Requires that the field come first in
`order_by`.
 - EQUAL: Equal.
 - OVERLAP: Overlap (have elements in common). Field and values must be lists.
 - NOT_EQUAL: Not Equal.

v1GetAvailableMetricsRequest.description:
Request for GetAvailableMetrics. No parameters required.
Returns the full catalog of queryable metrics with their schemas.

v1GetAvailableMetricsResponse.description: Response for GetAvailableMetrics. Contains the full catalog of metrics that can be queried.

metrics.description:
All available metrics with their schemas. Use this to discover valid metric names,
measures, and dimensions for building QueryMetricsRequest messages.

dimension.title: A list of dimensions to group by

excludeCombination.title: Supports excluding a list of combinations

v1GroupBy.title: Group By is a reducer for aggregating rows into the given dimensions

v1GroupResourceId.description:
A group of businesses identified by a hierarchical path.
Groups allow querying aggregate analytics across multiple business locations.

groupPathNodes.description: The ordered list of group path nodes from root to leaf (e.g., ["root-node", "child-node"]).

v1JWTResourceId.description: A JWT that encodes a specific user/business combination for authenticated end-user queries.

jwt.description: A signed JWT containing partner_id and business_id claims.

v1MarketId.description:
One or more market IDs. Markets are geographic or logical groupings of businesses within a partner.
When market_ids is empty, the query returns data across ALL markets for the partner (partner-wide query).

marketIds.description: The list of market IDs to query. An empty list means all markets for the partner.

measure.title: Select a measure field as-is without aggregation. Example: "scaled_stars"

v1MetricDescription.description:
Describes a single queryable metric, including its available measures, dimensions, and time field.
Use this information to construct valid QueryMetricsRequest messages.

description.description: A human-readable description of what this metric measures.

dimensions.title:
The dimension fields available for this metric. These can be used in QueryMetricsRequest.group_by
and QueryMetricsRequest.filter. Examples: "source_id", "rating", "account_group_id"

measures.title:
The measure fields available for this metric. These can be used in QueryMetricsRequest.measures
for selection or aggregation. Examples: "scaled_stars", "review_count", "likes"

name.title:
The unique identifier for this metric. Pass this as QueryMetricsRequest.metric_name.
Examples: "reviews", "activation_counts", "facebook_post_stats"

timeField.description:
The primary time field used for date range filtering and time alignment.
This is the field that alignment_period and time_range operate on.

v1MetricResult.description:
A single row of metric data. Results are hierarchically nested by the group_by dimensions.
The dimension value identifies which group this row belongs to, and measures contains the values
in the same order as the measures_schema in the parent ResourceMetricResult.

dimension.description:
The value of the dimension for this row (e.g., a date string, source ID, or rating value).
When alignment is used, the outermost dimension is the time bucket start timestamp.

measures.description:
The measure values for this row, in the same order as measures_schema.properties.
Each value corresponds to the property at the same index in the schema.

v1Metrics.description: A list of metric result rows.

v1QueryMetricsRequest.description:
QueryMetricsRequest queries a single named metric with full control over measures, dimensions,
time alignment, filtering, grouping, ordering, and pagination.

IMPORTANT: Call GetAvailableMetrics first to discover valid metric_name values and their
available measures, dimensions, and time fields.

## How to build a query

1. Set partner_id (required) and at least one resource_id to scope the query.
2. Set metric_name to one of the values returned by GetAvailableMetrics.
3. Use time_range (not the deprecated date_range) to define the time window.
4. Optionally set alignment + alignment_period to bucket results into time series
   (e.g., ALIGN_DELTA with CALENDAR_MONTH for monthly deltas).
5. Optionally add measures to select or aggregate specific fields.
   If omitted, all default measures for the metric are returned.
6. Optionally add group_by to break results down by dimensions.
7. Optionally add filter to narrow results (e.g., filter by source_id or rating).

## Common query patterns

### Get total reviews for a business over the last 90 days
{
  "partner_id": "PARTNER-ID",
  "metric_name": "reviews",
  "resource_ids": [{"business_id": {"business_id": "AG-XXXXXXXX"}}],
  "time_range": {"start": "2025-12-01T00:00:00Z", "end_exclusive": "2026-03-01T00:00:00Z"},
  "alignment": "ALIGN_DELTA",
  "alignment_period": {"calendar": "CALENDAR_MONTH"}
}

### Get review breakdown by star rating
{
  "partner_id": "PARTNER-ID",
  "metric_name": "reviews",
  "resource_ids": [{"business_id": {"business_id": "AG-XXXXXXXX"}}],
  "time_range": {"start": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "end": "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z"},
  "alignment": "ALIGN_DELTA",
  "alignment_period": {"calendar": "CALENDAR_MONTH"},
  "group_by": {"dimension": [{"dimension": "rating"}]}
}

### Get activation counts across all markets for a partner
{
  "partner_id": "PARTNER-ID",
  "metric_name": "activation_counts",
  "resource_ids": [{"market_id": {"market_ids": []}}],
  "time_range": {"start": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z", "end_exclusive": "2026-04-01T00:00:00Z"},
  "alignment": "ALIGN_EXACT",
  "alignment_period": {"calendar": "CALENDAR_MONTH"}
}

## Available metric categories

- Reviews & Reputation: "reviews", "all_reviews", "net_promoter_score", "multi_location_net_promoter_score"
- Social Media: "facebook_post_stats", "facebook_post_posts", "instagram_post_stats", "instagram_post_posts",
  "linkedin_post_stats", "linkedin_post_posts", "twitter_post_stats", "twitter_post_posts",
  "gmb_post_stats", "gmb_post_posts"
- Enhanced Social: "new_sm_facebook_post_stats_enhanced", "new_sm_instagram_post_stats_enhanced",
  "new_sm_linkedin_post_stats_enhanced", "new_sm_twitter_post_stats_enhanced"
- Listings: "listings", "listing_score", "listing_presence_over_time"
- Contacts & Conversations: "contact_source_v2", "contact_source_market", "conversation_source",
  "widget_visits", "inbox_messages"
- Accounts & Activation: "account_company", "account_group", "activation_counts",
  "activation_history", "activation_history_unaligned", "paid_activation_counts",
  "deactivation_reasons", "sm_account_group_editions"
- Connections: "social_connections"
- Sales: "sales_opportunities"

Call GetAvailableMetrics for the full up-to-date list with measures and dimensions.

cursor.description:
Optional. Pagination cursor from a previous response's ResourceMetricResult.cursor.
Pass an empty string or omit to start from the beginning.

limit.description:
Optional. Maximum number of result rows to return. 0 means no limit.
Use with order_by for "top N" queries (e.g., top 10 businesses by review count).

measures.description:
Optional. The measures to select or aggregate from the metric.
Each metric has a set of available measures (returned by GetAvailableMetrics).
If omitted, all default measures for the metric are returned.
Use Measure.measure for raw selection (e.g., "scaled_stars") or
Measure.aggregate for aggregations (e.g., AVG of "scaled_stars").

metricName.title:
Required. The name of the metric to query. Must be one of the names returned by GetAvailableMetrics.
Examples: "reviews", "activation_counts", "facebook_post_stats", "net_promoter_score"

partnerId.description: Required. The partner ID to scope the query to. All queries are scoped to a single partner.

resourceIds.description:
Required. At least one resource ID to scope the query to. Multiple resource IDs of the same type
can be provided to query across multiple businesses, groups, or markets in a single call.

v1QueryMetricsResponse.description: Response for QueryMetrics. Contains one ResourceMetricResult per requested resource ID.

metricResults.description: Results for each requested resource, in the same order as the request's resource_ids.

v1ResourceId.description:
ResourceId identifies the scope of a query. Exactly one resource type must be set.
Each resource type determines what data is returned and how it is aggregated.

v1ResourceMetricResult.description:
The result for a single resource ID in a QueryMetrics response.
Contains the data along with schemas describing the structure of measures and dimensions.

cursor.description:
Pagination cursor. Pass this value as QueryMetricsRequest.cursor to fetch the next page.
Empty when there are no more results.

v1SalesTeam.description: One or more sales team IDs for querying analytics scoped to sales team assignments.

salesTeamIds.description: The list of sales team IDs to query.

v1TypedValue.description: A message that can hold any of the supported value types.

dimension.description: The field to which to apply the operator.

v1UnaryFilter.title: A filter with a single operand

v1UnaryFilterOperator.description:
A unary operator.

 - OPERATOR_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified. This value must not be used.
 - IS_NAN: Test if a field is equal to NaN.
 - IS_NULL: Test if an expression evaluates to Null.
 - IS_NOT_NULL: Test if an expression evaluates to Not Null.

name.title:
Unique identifier for this property. Must be in snake case format.
Example(s): account_group_id, listing_id, company_name

properties.description:
Can only be specified if the Type supplied is a STRUCT.
Is the schema of the structured property.

repeated.description: Indicates if the field is repeated.

vendastatypesProperty.title: Property defines a single property for a schema

vendastatypesSchema.title: Schema describes the structure of data in a storage-agnostic way

scopes.business-app: Allows the application to manage all features of Business App

post.description:
IMPORTANT: Always call this first before calling QueryMetrics. The response tells you:
- Valid metric_name values to use in QueryMetrics
- Available measures for each metric (usable in QueryMetricsRequest.measures)
- Available dimensions for each metric (usable in QueryMetricsRequest.group_by and filter)
- The time field used for date range filtering and alignment

The response is not scoped to a partner — it returns the global set of available metrics.
Some metrics may return empty results for a given partner if no data exists.

requestBody.description:
Request for GetAvailableMetrics. No parameters required.
Returns the full catalog of queryable metrics with their schemas.

post.description:
This is the primary analytics endpoint. It supports flexible querying of any available metric
with full control over measures, dimensions, time alignment, filtering, grouping, and pagination.

IMPORTANT: Call GetAvailableMetrics first to discover valid metric names and their schemas.

## Quick start

1. Call GetAvailableMetrics to get the list of metric names, measures, and dimensions.
2. Pick a metric_name (e.g., "reviews", "activation_counts").
3. Set partner_id and at least one resource_id (typically a business_id).
4. Set time_range with start and end_exclusive timestamps.
5. Set alignment (usually ALIGN_DELTA) and alignment_period (e.g., CALENDAR_MONTH).

## Example: Monthly review counts for a business

{
  "partner_id": "PARTNER-ID",
  "metric_name": "reviews",
  "resource_ids": [{"business_id": {"business_id": "AG-XXXXXXXX"}}],
  "time_range": {"start": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "end_exclusive": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"},
  "alignment": "ALIGN_DELTA",
  "alignment_period": {"calendar": "CALENDAR_MONTH"}
}

requestBody.description:
QueryMetricsRequest queries a single named metric with full control over measures, dimensions,
time alignment, filtering, grouping, ordering, and pagination.

IMPORTANT: Call GetAvailableMetrics first to discover valid metric_name values and their
available measures, dimensions, and time fields.

## How to build a query

1. Set partner_id (required) and at least one resource_id to scope the query.
2. Set metric_name to one of the values returned by GetAvailableMetrics.
3. Use time_range (not the deprecated date_range) to define the time window.
4. Optionally set alignment + alignment_period to bucket results into time series
   (e.g., ALIGN_DELTA with CALENDAR_MONTH for monthly deltas).
5. Optionally add measures to select or aggregate specific fields.
   If omitted, all default measures for the metric are returned.
6. Optionally add group_by to break results down by dimensions.
7. Optionally add filter to narrow results (e.g., filter by source_id or rating).

## Common query patterns

### Get total reviews for a business over the last 90 days
{
  "partner_id": "PARTNER-ID",
  "metric_name": "reviews",
  "resource_ids": [{"business_id": {"business_id": "AG-XXXXXXXX"}}],
  "time_range": {"start": "2025-12-01T00:00:00Z", "end_exclusive": "2026-03-01T00:00:00Z"},
  "alignment": "ALIGN_DELTA",
  "alignment_period": {"calendar": "CALENDAR_MONTH"}
}

### Get review breakdown by star rating
{
  "partner_id": "PARTNER-ID",
  "metric_name": "reviews",
  "resource_ids": [{"business_id": {"business_id": "AG-XXXXXXXX"}}],
  "time_range": {"start": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "end": "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z"},
  "alignment": "ALIGN_DELTA",
  "alignment_period": {"calendar": "CALENDAR_MONTH"},
  "group_by": {"dimension": [{"dimension": "rating"}]}
}

### Get activation counts across all markets for a partner
{
  "partner_id": "PARTNER-ID",
  "metric_name": "activation_counts",
  "resource_ids": [{"market_id": {"market_ids": []}}],
  "time_range": {"start": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z", "end_exclusive": "2026-04-01T00:00:00Z"},
  "alignment": "ALIGN_EXACT",
  "alignment_period": {"calendar": "CALENDAR_MONTH"}
}

## Available metric categories

- Reviews & Reputation: "reviews", "all_reviews", "net_promoter_score", "multi_location_net_promoter_score"
- Social Media: "facebook_post_stats", "facebook_post_posts", "instagram_post_stats", "instagram_post_posts",
  "linkedin_post_stats", "linkedin_post_posts", "twitter_post_stats", "twitter_post_posts",
  "gmb_post_stats", "gmb_post_posts"
- Enhanced Social: "new_sm_facebook_post_stats_enhanced", "new_sm_instagram_post_stats_enhanced",
  "new_sm_linkedin_post_stats_enhanced", "new_sm_twitter_post_stats_enhanced"
- Listings: "listings", "listing_score", "listing_presence_over_time"
- Contacts & Conversations: "contact_source_v2", "contact_source_market", "conversation_source",
  "widget_visits", "inbox_messages"
- Accounts & Activation: "account_company", "account_group", "activation_counts",
  "activation_history", "activation_history_unaligned", "paid_activation_counts",
  "deactivation_reasons", "sm_account_group_editions"
- Connections: "social_connections"
- Sales: "sales_opportunities"

Call GetAvailableMetrics for the full up-to-date list with measures and dimensions.
